Drag a model onto the canvas, or tap a model then tap canvas to place. Snap grid is on by default. Resize range supports very small to large scaling.
Game copyright stays with Jason Groce. Level creator credit will be saved from your creator name. Please consider donating to the Kennard Volunteer Fire Department.

Builder Help

Core Build Flow

  • Set your Level Name, then place models from the Asset Library onto the canvas to start building.
  • Use the left Asset Library to pick or upload images, then click canvas once to place.
  • Use Layers to move, scale, flip, lock physics, set burnability, and tune burn boxes.
  • The fire truck starts on the Left. Change Truck Side to Right anytime.
  • Burn boxes start on Easy so every burnable object works immediately; raise box tiers to Medium/Hard if wanted.
  • Use Test Difficulty + Quick Play to test easy/medium/hard behavior.
  • Use Events for tanker timing, tornado timing, and bonus vehicles. Then use the Export buttons to save, package, or share.

What Each Export/Test Button Does

  • Load Current Level: loads the currently active game level into builder for editing.
  • New Blank: starts a fresh blank builder level and keeps your current settings defaults.
  • Save Level (This Browser): saves this level only in this browser on this computer.
  • Load Saved Level: opens a level you previously saved in this browser.
  • Download Level Package: downloads one portable level JSON you can import later.
  • Import Level Package: loads a packaged level JSON into Builder.
  • Copy Package JSON: copies the level package text to clipboard for sharing.
  • Download Source Submission File: creates `.kvfdsubmission.json` for source-slot updates.
  • Print Level Summary: prints level details and integration notes.
  • Test Level: launches quick test play using selected test difficulty.
  • Save To Game Slot: writes to local browser game slot override on this computer only.
  • Download Offline Game (This Level): exports game + images + this level with play links.
  • Download Offline Game (No Custom Level): exports base game + images without this level.

Permanent Source Update Workflow (Local Repo -> Online)

Use this when you want your builder changes to become real source files and then publish online after testing.

  • 1) Build and test level in builder until it plays correctly.
  • 2) Click Download Source Submission File and save the `.kvfdsubmission.json` file.
  • 3) Run tools\\apply-builder-submission-local-qa.cmd for local apply + test + Commit/Undo buttons.
  • 4) Select your submission file. The tool updates local source slot data in `mobile-deluxe-23.html`, waits for testing, then commits if approved.
  • 5) Optional fast path: run tools\\apply-builder-submission-and-publish.cmd for direct one-step apply + push.
  • 6) Refresh the site/game and verify the slot behavior online.

Important: Save To Game Slot is for fast local testing. Submission + local QA script is the safe permanent update path.

Do you want instructions?

Choose Show Instructions for a quick guided tour with arrows and click-through tips, or Go To Builder to start editing immediately.

KENNARD FIRE DEFENDER DELUXE

Choose Your Rank

Why This Game Exists

This game was created to bring fun and entertainment to our community while sharing important fire-safety reminders between levels. It was also made with the intent of raising funds for new tools, equipment upgrades, and ultimately building a modern fire station that will better serve our growing community for the next 100 years. The Kennard Volunteer Fire Department was founded in 1924, and after a powerful tornado devastated much of our town in 1974, destroying the school and requiring the town hall and fire station to be rebuilt, we have continued to grow. Over the decades our needs have grown too, and today’s significantly larger and longer fire trucks no longer fit inside our current station.

If you would like to support the Kennard Volunteer Fire Department:

Email: vfd@kennardin.com

Checks payable to: Town of Kennard
Mail to: 100 N Main St, P.O. Box 167, Kennard, IN 47351
Please include: “For Volunteer Fire Department” in the memo.

Thank you for playing and helping keep our volunteers strong.
Enjoy the game! - Jason Groce - Kennard, Indiana

Thank You for Playing

This game was created to bring fun and entertainment to our community while sharing fire-safety reminders between levels. It was also built to help raise funds for new tools, equipment upgrades, and the long-term goal of building a modern fire station that can serve our growing community for the next 100 years.

The Kennard Volunteer Fire Department was founded in 1924. In 1974, a powerful tornado devastated much of our town, destroyed the school, and required major rebuilding of the town hall and fire station. Our community kept moving forward, and today our needs have grown again. Modern fire apparatus are larger and longer, and our current station no longer fits the equipment we rely on to protect the town.

If you would like to support the Kennard Volunteer Fire Department:

Email: vfd@kennardin.com

Checks payable to: Town of Kennard
Mail to: 100 N Main St, P.O. Box 167, Kennard, IN 47351
Please include: For Volunteer Fire Department in the memo.

Thank you for playing and helping keep our volunteers strong.
I hope you enjoyed the game.
Jason Groce - Kennard, Indiana
